steps.text: Some letters are extra special — they're called vowels. Every word needs at least one! Find a and trace it!
steps.feedback: a! You'll find this in almost every word. Now find e!
e! The most common letter in English. Now find i!
i! Don't forget the dot on top. Now find o!
o! A perfect circle. Last one — find u!
u! Like a little cup. a, e, i, o, u — all five vowels!

steps.text: These letters all start the same way — with a round curve going anti-clockwise. Find c and trace it — start at the top and go round!
steps.feedback: c! That curving stroke is the key. Now find o — all the way round!
o! A full circle, anti-clockwise. Now find a — it starts just like c!
a! Round first, then the straight bit down. Now find d — round first, then tall!
d! Same round start, but the line goes up high. Last one — find g!
g! Round like the others, then the tail dips below. You mastered the round letters!

steps.text: The alphabet has a special order. Let's trace the very first five letters! Find a — the first letter!
steps.feedback: a! Right at the start. Now find b!
b! A tall letter — the line goes up high. Now find c!
c! The round one. Now find d!
d! Tall again, but faces the other way to b. Last one — find e!
e! a, b, c, d, e — five in a row!

steps.text: Numbers have shapes too — let's trace them in order! Find 1 and trace it!
steps.feedback: 1! One straight line. Now find 2!
2! A curve and a slide. Now find 3!
3! Two bumps facing right. Now find 4!
4! Down, across, then tall. Last one — find 5!
5! Straight down, belly out, hat on top. You wrote them all!

steps.text: You know 1 to 5 — now let's trace 6 all the way to 10! We use 0 for the zero in 10. Find 6 and trace it!
steps.feedback: 6! Curves round and closes at the bottom. Now find 7!
7! Across the top, then a diagonal line down. Now find 8!
8! Two loops on top of each other. Now find 9!
9! A circle on top, then a tail going down. Last one — find 0!
0! A full oval — the zero that makes 10. You can write all ten digits!
